title: Feast and famine — a journal prompt set
subtitle: Three tight questions for the money pattern of big inflows followed by long dry months.
excerpt: A three-prompt set on the feast-and-famine money cycle. Drawn from Day 4 of MONEY and the Week 1 reflection in The Money Journal. Fifteen minutes total — five minutes per prompt, against a timer.
sourceNotes: Theme adapted from Day 4 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Free-write style: The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The three-prompt structure (action, feeling, inheritance) is original to the homemade.education library.

practice.whenToUse: Use when a big inflow has just arrived and the panic of it disappearing has surfaced, or in the middle of a dry month when the next inflow doesn't yet have a date.

content.text: A three-prompt set on the feast-and-famine money cycle — the pattern where a big inflow arrives, then disappears, and the next one doesn't have a date. Theme drawn from Day 4 of Rebecca's MONEY program; format follows the free-write style she uses across The Money Journal.
New to this style of journal practice? Read
first — five minutes per prompt against a timer, no editing, keep the pen moving.
1. The last time the money came in, what did I do with it?
Be specific. The actual transactions, in order, with what feeling. Don't soften the relief-spends or the panic-spends.
2. What did I expect to feel when the money arrived, and what did I actually feel?
The gap between the expected feeling and the actual one is often where the cycle holds. Write the gap.
3. Who in my life would have called a steady, large, sitting bank balance a problem?
Not who would have envied it. Who would have called it a problem. Write the names. Write the lines they used.

Theme: Day 4 of MONEY (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "End the feast/famine cycle". Free-write method: The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The three-prompt walk (action, feeling, inheritance) is original to the homemade.education library; the same structure works for other money cycles too.
